sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, text_col VARCHAR, num_col DOUBLE, date_col DATE);
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (1, 'bananabread', 3.14, '2023-01-01'), (2, NULL, 2.71, '2023-02-01'), (3, 'anabanana', 0.0, NULL);
SELECT id, text_col, regexp_full_match(text_col, '(an)*') AS matches, num_col ** 2 AS squared FROM test_table WHERE num_col IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, AVG(num_col) AS avg_num FROM test_table GROUP BY id HAVING AVG(num_col) > (SELECT AVG(num_col) FROM test_table WHERE text_col IS NOT NULL);
SELECT id, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY text_col) AS count_per_text FROM test_table ORDER BY count_per_text DESC;
SELECT id, text_col FROM test_table t1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM test_table t2 WHERE regexp_full_match(t2.text_col, 'banana') AND t1.num_col ** 2 = t2.num_col);
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE temp_table AS SELECT id, text_col, num_col FROM test_table WHERE regexp_full_match(text_col, '^(an)*');
SELECT t1.id, t1.text_col, t2.num_col ** 2 FROM test_table t1 JOIN temp_table t2 ON t1.id = t2.id WHERE t2.num_col IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, IFNULL(text_col, 'default_text') AS safe_text FROM test_table WHERE text_col IS NOT NULL OR num_col > 1.0;
SELECT regexp_full_match(text_col, '^b.*(an)*$') AS full_match, count(*) FROM test_table GROUP BY full_match HAVING full_match IS NULL;
